description_long: This object is a collection of long, pole-like melee weapons, each featuring a distinct blade or pointed tip at one end and a wooden or metal shaft. The set includes various historical polearms such as halberds, spears, tridents, and axes, each with unique blade shapes—some are curved, others are straight, and a few have multiple prongs or axe heads. The shafts are predominantly wooden, with some featuring metal reinforcements or decorative bands, and the blades are metallic, ranging from matte black to steel gray. Handles are generally straight, with some wrapped or textured for grip, and a few weapons have visible crossguards or guards between the blade and shaft. The overall appearance is utilitarian with minimal ornamentation, suggesting practical use rather than ceremonial display. The weapons vary in length but are all designed for two-handed use, typical of polearms found in historical or fantasy urban settings.
description: A set of long, pole-like melee weapons with various blade shapes and wooden shafts, including spears, halberds, axes, and tridents. The blades are metallic and the handles are mostly straight, some with guards or textured grips.
description_view_0: A frontal view showing the full array of polearms, highlighting the diversity of blade shapes and the length of each weapon.
description_view_1: A side view showing the thin profile of the weapons, making the blades and shafts appear as narrow lines.
description_view_2: A rear view displaying the backs of the blades and the shafts, with the curvature and thickness of some blades more visible.
description_view_3: A side view from the opposite direction, again emphasizing the slimness of the weapons and the alignment of the shafts.
attribute_blade_type: varied; includes straight, curved, double-edged, single-edged, and pronged blades
attribute_handle_design: straight wooden shafts, some with leather wrapping or metal bands, minimal ergonomic shaping
